title: lotodes biflorum
intro: Lotodes biflorum, also known as water violet or marsh violet, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the family Haloragaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids and flavonoids content. The health benefits of Lotodes biflorum include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and expectorant. Herbal preparations of Lotodes biflorum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and creams for topical application.
